title: Supermarket habits
description: Going for milk and leaving with a full trolley, the self-checkout that always needs assistance and those loyalty points you'll never use — supermarket life is universal!

questions.text: Name something people go in to buy but always leave with more.

answers.text: milk and bread — but also a full basket
wine — bought three instead of one
snacks because of the offers
one item from the middle aisle
a dessert that wasn't planned

questions.text: What do people say about the self-checkout machine?

answers.text: unexpected item in bagging area — always
why is it never working properly
where is the human checkout when you need it
faster when you know how to use it
I hate having to wait for approval for alcohol

questions.text: Name something people do to avoid a long queue.

answers.text: scan the checkouts and pick the shortest
go at a quieter time of day
use self-checkout even with a big shop
abandon the trolley and come back later
order online instead

questions.text: Name something always bought on impulse in the supermarket.

answers.text: chocolate near the checkout
something on offer that seemed a deal
a magazine or newspaper
a meal deal or deal bundle
something seasonal you don't normally buy

questions.text: What do people say when they forget their shopping list?

answers.text: I'll just try to remember — and fail
I'll call someone at home to check
I'll buy what looks right and hope
I definitely forgot something important
I'm coming back tomorrow anyway

questions.text: Name something people compare prices on in the supermarket.

answers.text: branded vs own-brand products
price per 100g to find best value
multibuy offers vs single items
fresh vs frozen vegetables
different sizes of the same product

questions.text: What do people say about loyalty card points?

answers.text: I never remember to use them
I've got loads saved up but never spend them
I always scan even for small shops
I don't bother — it's not worth it
finally used mine for a big shop

questions.text: Name something supermarkets do that people find manipulative but works.

answers.text: place essentials at the back of the store
big deals and yellow price tags everywhere
bakery smells pumped near the entrance
checkout sweets at eye level for kids
buy one get one free on things you don't need

questions.text: Name something people judge in other people's trolleys.

answers.text: too much junk food
lots of alcohol
all branded nothing own-brand
only ready meals — can't cook?
no vegetables in sight

questions.text: Name something that makes supermarket shopping miserable.

answers.text: it's packed on a Saturday afternoon
they've rearranged the whole layout again
your favourite thing is out of stock
the bags cost extra and you forgot yours
you're hungry and everything looks good
